evidence.text:
Russian President Dmitry Medvedev and Bono met in Medvedev├óΓé¼Γäós Black Sea resort on Tuesday to discuss music (Medvedev is a huge rock n├óΓé¼Γäó roll fan) and charity.
“You are doing important things, because taking care of people is not only a job for politicians,” said Medvedev
The duo also discussed problems in Africa, specifically the desperate measures being taken to stop the spread of HIV/AIDS and bring the population out of destitute, wide-spread poverty.
Bono wasn├óΓé¼Γäót shy to suggest things to the President, and told Medvedev that spending 40 cents per day could eliminate the transfer of HIV from mother to child by 2015.
Medvedev obviously has a lot of respect for the singer. “The charity work that you and your colleagues do has brought you respect all over the world,” said the President.
U2 will be playing a concert in Russia as a part of their ├óΓé¼┼ô360├óΓé¼┬¥ world tour.

evidence.page_title: Medvedev, U2's Bono discuss Russia, health, musicians - Trend.Az
evidence.title: Medvedev, U2's Bono discuss Russia, health, musicians
evidence.description: On Tuesday, Bono met with Russian President Dmitry Medvedev ahead of the U2 concert due in Moscow on August 25 at the presidential summer residence in the Black Sea resort city of Sochi, RIA Novosti reported.
evidence.text:
On Tuesday, Bono met with Russian President Dmitry Medvedev ahead of the U2 concert due in Moscow on August 25 at the presidential summer residence in the Black Sea resort city of Sochi, RIA Novosti reported.
Bono said he had arrived in Russia like a student, saying he had a lot to learn about Russia.
Bono also told the Russian president that they have some differences in taste since Medvedev is a Deep Purple fan and Bono is a Led Zeppelin fan.
"That's not completely true, I love Led Zeppelin," Medvedev told the musician.
However, Medvedev said he did not want to restrict his conversation with Bono to only music.
Bono actively works in fighting HIV and AIDS around the world. He also organizes many charity events along with the other members of U2 as well as other celebrities.
"This social activity you are engaged in, you and your colleagues, gets great respect from people," Medvedev said. "You do important things because taking care of people is not only a matter of politics," he continued.
"Each of us should actually contribute into, as it seems, [relieving the] very complex problems of health, the fight against poverty, cleaning up the aftermaths of crises," the Russian leader said. "And the fact that you are actively engaged in this, I think, is cool."
On Tuesday, Medvedev also said he will support the leader of U2 Bono in his efforts to fight HIV/AIDS.
After the conversation, Medvedev and Bono took a stroll along the Black Sea coast.
